metadata.title: Points to Consider for Assessors - New Factor VIII and Factor IX Products: Potency Determination for Labelling and Assays for Testing Post-Infusion Samples

rules.rule_text: Comprehensive potency data on new FVIII and FIX products measured relative to the WHO International Standard (IS) Concentrate must be provided in quality section 3.2.S.3 of the dossier, using a variety of potency methods including one-stage clotting (with different APTT reagents) and chromogenic methods.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comprehensive data on the potency characteristics of new FVIII and FIX products measured relative to the WHO International Standard (IS) Concentrate (FVIII or FIX), as described in the SSC/ISTH recommendations ... should be provided in the quality part of the dossier (3.2.S.3). This should include data from a variety of potency methods (e.g. the one-stage clotting method using a range of APTT reagents with different composition and the chromogenic method using different kits).

rules.rule_text: Potency data relative to the WHO IS Plasma (FVIII and FIX) must also be available to anticipate issues in post-infusion testing where a plasma standard may be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potency data on the product, calculated relative to the WHO IS Plasma (FVIII and FIX) using a variety of methods, should also be available in order to anticipate issues in post-infusion testing where a plasma standard may be used.

rules.rule_text: The assay design for potency characterisation must allow assessment of statistical validity of testing relative to the WHO IS Concentrate (e.g. parallelism of dose-response relationships). Valid tests relative to WHO IS are required to support IU labelling.
rules.verbatim_quote: The assay design used for potency characterisation should allow assessment of the statistical validity of testing the product relative to the WHO IS Concentrate (FVIII and FIX) (e.g. parallelism of dose-response relationships). Valid tests of product relative to the WHO IS Concentrate are required to support potency labelling in International Units (IU).
rules.exceptions: Where valid tests are not possible, consideration should be given to labelling in 'product-specific units' (EMA/CHMP/BWP/85290/2012).

rules.rule_text: When significant potency discrepancies exist across assay methods, an internal reference standard must be established for product potency labelling, with value assigned in IU relative to the WHO IS Concentrate using the chosen method. Its stability must be monitored and replacement strategy described.
rules.verbatim_quote: An internal reference standard should be established for product potency labelling and value assigned in IU, relative to the WHO IS Concentrate, using the chosen method. Details of method and reagents used should be provided in the dossier. The stability of the internal reference standard should be monitored and the replacement strategy clearly described.

rules.rule_text: If significant potency discrepancies exist depending on assay and reference standards used, the appropriateness of the potency chosen for labelling must be demonstrated and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a product shows significant potency discrepancies depending on the assay and reference standards used, it should be demonstrated and justified that the potency chosen for labelling is appropriate.

rules.rule_text: When the WHO IS Concentrate is replaced, continuity of potency assignment of the internal reference standard must be checked. Manufacturers must outline the procedure for handling WHO IS changes in sections 3.2.S.5 and/or 3.2.P.6 of the dossier.
rules.verbatim_quote: When the WHO IS Concentrate is replaced, the continuity of the potency assignment of the internal reference standard should be checked. The manufacturer should outline the procedure in case of a change of the WHO IS in the dossier (3.2.S.5 and/or 3.2.P.6).

rules.rule_text: Clinical assessors must check whether the company has investigated the effect of different test methods and reagents on measurement of representative post-infusion samples. Appropriate measures must be addressed in the SmPC and RMP.
rules.verbatim_quote: In liaison with the quality assessor it should be checked whether the company has investigated the effect of different test methods and reagents on the measurement of representative post-infusion samples. Appropriate measures to be addressed in the SmPC and the risk management plan (RMP) should be considered.

rules.rule_text: The RMP must discuss strategy to inform/train clinical laboratory users about the risk of discrepant assay results in clinical monitoring. It should include information on suitable/unsuitable methods and reagents for post-infusion testing.
rules.verbatim_quote: The RMP is the appropriate place to discuss the strategy to inform/train users and clinical laboratories that may be needed to address the risk of discrepant assay results in clinical monitoring, where the quality/clinical documentation has highlighted that significant discrepancies can occur ... If applicable the RMP should consider: Information on suitable and unsuitable methods and reagents for post-infusion testing
rules.exceptions: Only if applicable based on quality/clinical documentation.

rules.rule_text: The RMP should consider availability of a product-specific laboratory reference material where use of a local reference material is not advised, and information on correction factors to adjust measured potency.
rules.verbatim_quote: Availability of a product-specific laboratory reference material where the use of a local reference material is not advised ... Information on correction factors to adjust measured potency

rules.rule_text: Manufacturers should collaborate with Proficiency Testing Schemes for evaluation of product and post-infusion sample testing in the wider community following marketing authorisation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers could be encouraged to initiate collaboration with Proficiency Testing Schemes in the evaluation of product / post-infusion sample testing in the wider community following marketing authorisation.
